meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the no portfolio problem. An intermediate-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping a functional SaaS application, not just theory. Using a SaaS starter kit allows you to bypass boilerplate code, focusing your effort on unique feature logic. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you can deploy a professional-grade production app in hours, not months.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank cursor, the weight of a dozen 'To-Do' list tutorials heavy on your conscience. You know the theory, you understand the syntax, but your GitHub profile is a graveyard of abandoned projects that prove nothing to a potential employer. The industry doesn't care about your coursework; it cares about your ability to ship value.

This is the threshold where students become engineers. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you transform from a tutorial-follower into a product builder. This case study strips away the fluff, showing you how to leverage AI-augmented workflows to build a production-ready application that turns your 'no portfolio' problem into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Architecting the Stack
steps.description: Define the core feature set of your SaaS to ensure it solves a real-world problem rather than mimicking a classroom exercise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular architecture diagram for a subscription-based SaaS using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe, focusing on clean separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a project structure that follows clean architecture principles for a SaaS starter'.

steps.title: Implementing Authentication and Database
steps.description: Set up secure user management and relational data models, the backbone of any professional SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust authentication schema and database migration scripts for a multi-tenant SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure Supabase auth and create the initial users and billing tables'.

steps.title: Integrating the Billing Engine
steps.description: Connect Stripe to handle subscriptions, which demonstrates your capability to manage complex financial logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler for subscription lifecycle events including upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the Stripe webhook service and connect it to the user subscription status table'.

steps.title: Building the Core Value Proposition
steps.description: Develop the unique 'hook' of your application—the feature that differentiates your SaaS from generic templates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a high-performance API route that processes user input and returns AI-enhanced insights using the OpenAI SDK.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Build the core feature module and integrate it with the main dashboard UI'.

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automate your deployment process to ensure your code is always production-ready, mimicking real engineering team workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and automated deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup a CI/CD pipeline for automated testing and deployment'.

pro_tips: Focus on 'shipping' over 'perfecting'—a live URL with a bug is worth more than a perfect local repo.
Document your architectural decisions in the README; employers value the 'why' behind your code.
Use mock data early to build the UI, then swap in the real API calls once the structure is solid.
Always include a 'Request a Feature' button; it shows you understand product-led growth.
Treat your repository as a product: write professional commit messages and maintain a clean change log.
content.next_steps: After completing your deployment, create a 60-second video demo showcasing the user journey and pin it to the top of your LinkedIn and GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no portfolio' label define your career trajectory. You have the tools and the blueprint to build something that commands respect from hiring managers and founders alike. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
